reviews.review_description: Played this on PSVR2. Had to get the quest version so I can play on the go. Great drop in drop out game. Fun tactics for high scores.

reviews.review_title: Great Game/Update Game No Longer Works
reviews.review_description:
Was a 5 star game, now it no longer works. Trapped in the elevator and can't start game! Sad because I almost completed all the puzzles :(

(Original Review) Fun game in both story mode with puzzles to solve or just game mode. Really polished game, places to explore, funny and sometimes rude robot boss who helps you out, feels like you're throwing a real ball....just perfect!

developer_response.body: Hi, sorry to hear about your bad experience. Could you send a more detailed description of your issue to support@tunermaxx.com? Nothing has changed about the game in terms of the software recently, so it's surprising to hear it would suddenly stop working, so we'd have to investigate to fix the problem. Thanks!

reviews.review_description:
I had to try the game since it seemed like a possibly nice light game to play. Overall the game is nice so far, even if using the broom is awkward for some reason (though still gets the job done well enough). The biggest thing making me question the game's quality are the throwing physics, something that's never that great AT BEST in VR titles. Especially when you need precision as you do in a game like this.
There's nothing that even helps alleviate this common VR frustration like throwing lines or the like to help line up throws. Add to that with everything being at an angle AND being very limited on your time thanks to more balls always coming (absolutely GAME BREAKING unless you spend all of your CC on ball catchers...) and the game is seemingly  unplayable andca test of frustration for me. The opposite of why I got it in the first place.

So I'll try again, but really have no idea if I'm goingbto keep thisone..

developer_response.body: Hi there, sorry to hear about your frustration, but there must be something wrong here, as the throwing should be very accurate, at least with some practice. There are some tips on throwing in the tutorial video station in the basement. And make sure you don't have other apps running at the same time that could affect performance while playing, like recording video etc. Please get in touch via support@tunermaxx.com if we can assist further.

reviews.review_description: This game is underrated. Rainbow Reactor: Fusion offers a cozy and accessible VR experience in a single-player campaign puzzler. Keep the reactor from exploding and have a bunch of fun while doing it. 5/5 in my opinion - this game is wholesome, quirky, and worthy of a spot in your library.

reviews.review_title: Loving this game, have a question
reviews.review_description: Where in gods name do you find the water for the steam gun? I have been looking for days and I know it’s so simple because she tells me I should know where it is and I don’t. So can somebody please help me ￼

developer_response.body: Hi, glad to hear you like the game and congrats on making it so far in the story! Sorry about the confusion regarding the water, I guess the magic word is "hot" water here - you get it from the heater, there's a little water basin on its right hand side. Just dip the gun in there for a moment and you're good to go :D

reviews.review_description:
I really should read the negative reviews sometimes cos now it turns out I wasn’t the only player feeling the effects of motion sickness. I’ll try again later using the suggestions made by the developer, but for right now I need a bucket and a bed. Hopefully I’ll be able to move around easily like in “The Room vr”. 
From what I could play, the game started off well. I managed to solve one or two problems in story mode without difficulty, but having to search and find items caused me to move around a lot and that’s what done me in. But I’ll be back to try again.

developer_response.body: So sorry to hear you got motion sickness from playing! I'm also somewhat prone to it so I can relate - it's a nasty experience. Hopefully, using teleportation mode or choosing slow walking speed can help you in future play sessions. For more tips, you could have a look at our downloadable manual: https://tunermaxx.com/support/

reviews.review_description:
Edit: READ FIRST:::
Thank you for the response! I laughed so hard when i moved in the game and had to sit down 😂. Boyfriend did too!
Thank you for clearing that up!!!! Editing my review and adding the 5th star. I thought it was completely reset and was so tired I just quit the game and went to bed😂.

This is the 1st VR game that I've played that has movement. I just got the VR set a few days ago. Not gonna lie made me feel a bit queasy when I 1st moved and I was standing I quickly sat down, it was that or I was going to fall on the floor😂. I didn't make it very far as I was balancing out moving in game and not falling in real life. I love this game already. It truly feels like you're in the game, even the movement that made my brain and legs disconnect😂. That said I docked a star because when I went to play it the other night, my progression wasn't saved so it started me off from the beginning. I had just gotten off a 15 hr shift at work and just wanted to enjoy a few minutes of the game. Couldn't bring myself to start over even though I wasn't very far in. Waiting for my next day off to try it again.

developer_response.body: Hey there, thank you for the nice review, and its good to see more people come into VR and enjoy it! Moving in VR can generally be uncomfortable before you have your "VR legs", so it's definitely good to get used to it in small doses and stop if you start feeling weird. BUT you can also try "teleportation-style" movement in the game, which is less stressful. In the "VR Settings" menu in the main menu (and also in the wrist menu you can bring up with the X or A button) you can choose wether you want "free locomotion" or teleportation and also choose your walking speed from slow, medium or fast. Also, the mini-joystick makes you move slower when you do not press it all the way. And do not worry about losing your progress, it is always saved, but you start from the main entrance every time, it's just as if you had just returned to work, but everything will be exactly as you left it. In case you want to just have a quick round of ball throwing, you can also choose "Arcade Mode" in the main menu and get right to it. As a last tip, on our website tunermaxx.com/support/ you can download a free manual for the game. Thanks again :D

reviews.review_description: Such a cool game with great throw feel / mechanics. In many ways it hits the sweetspot of VR gaming. Story mode is just a perfect blend of exploration and humor. Figures Dottie made me clean floors on a saturday afternoon.. Most played game in our family, and to be honest I returned one of the top seller games (Pop1), this was a superior experience. Now if I could just get some extras from dancemoves done while throwing the chromaballs.. maybe in the next update?

reviews.review_title: Variety of matching colours plus more.
reviews.review_description: Nice game that I bought yesterday and have played for about 4 hours so far. Nice colour match game to power the reactors. Clearing the power plant. A nice little story. Great price and please support this title it is real fun.

reviews.review_title: Fun little game, very much built for VR
reviews.review_description:
This is no Half-Life Alyx, bug nobody expects that. The game is quirky at times and the pacing is sometimes a little slow. But the mechanics are very well thought out. There is surprisingly little jank, and everything is done the true VR way, grabbing things and moving them, no flatscreen-inspired button presses in sight. Mist importantly: I suck at throwing in VR. Which is a major component of the game. But it was still fun, and the throwing worked a lot better than in many much more expensive games.

Overall, yes, I can recommend this as a fun pastime.

I 've got so much game with my Quest, i 've never pay attention for this game. But today 21/12/2022
I decided to try it, what a great surprise, i really love it. I Hope devs don't give up this game and still update it

I don’t know what’s wrong but, I bought the ball holder but they won’t open for reactor 4. I also bought ball speed but, I can’t use it because they’re coming too fast because my back holder’s not working and not I don’t have enough money to slow the balls down. I don’t have any more task to complete for money, so I’m just stuck

developer_response.body: Hi there, first of all, thanks for playing! You can turn mods off and on in the tablet menu, so you should be able to simply set the ball speed back to normal. And there should always be ways to make more money by cleaning splats, destroying boxes and turning on "video boards" by unscrewing their fuse boxes and inserting the missing fuses. If you have any more questions, write to support@tunermaxx.com. You can also download a manual for the game at http://tunermaxx.com/support/

reviews.review_description:
Used to play it on Steam and was so happy that it came to Quest. I love the game, very addictive but will need some work done. When playing level 5 or 6, the game glitches, either I don't get any more balls or Dotty thinks the game is still going when I finished the round already.  Hopefully an update will fix this, then I would easily give it 5 stars.

Edit: developers are on the ball :) 
The game is the type of game that was missing on the Quest

developer_response.body: Thanks again for your help in tracking down this bug! We were able to find & eliminate it thanks to your description and the update is now available for all players :D

reviews.review_description: It has a great atmosphere an is challenging in one row. Great german and english synchro with funny videos on the start and tutorial stations im curios in what direction it goes next time i play

I am really enjoying this game.  it's a match 3 game where you toss the colored balls into a kind of honeycomb panel in front of you instead of clicking them like on a pc.  The physics feel right, the music is enjoyable and the game is cute without being cutesy.  The pace of gameplay is not frantic or really stressful but I have not found it too easy either, I think the pace is about perfect.

As far as the graphics go the pictures do not do the game justice, it is much nicer in person.  I do hope as the levels go on we get a change of scene to make the environment feel more alive.

I had a bit of trouble finding the leave game option in the pop up panel but over-all am a fan of the panel design choice. 

If you like match 3 games at all i think you will love this.

P.S. I love the Disco Sheep! Baa!

Devs:  Please add a snap to center option to get yourself into place quickly if you happen to move yourself accidentally mid level.  Also, The start button should be reachable after you have yourself in your preferred game play position.

developer_response.body: Thank you kindly for the review and your suggestions! We'll see if we can already offer up a centering option in our first planned update.

reviews.review_description:
The drift is so bad on my controllers on the original oculus that I can't even walk in a straight line. I tried contacting customer support and after a bunch of useless suggestions including a factory reset, nothing was suggested other than buying new controllers which they don't even manufacturer anymore. You would think they would treat their first adopters better. Oh well, there aren't too many games I can play anymore but I do enjoy the few that I can. Unfortunately I'm going to need a refund, just another frustrating day with the original oculus.

Update:
I really appreciate the suggestion of trying the toothpick trick but it didn't work for me. However, it did send me in the right direction: I found a YouTube video of a guy using cotton swabs and isopropyl alcohol to fix his drift problem with the oculus quest2 controllers and it worked great. I repurchased your game and I'm really enjoying it. It is quite challenging later on but that's a good thing. I really appreciate the devs willingness to try to help me and was happy to repurchase the game. 
Thanks!!

developer_response.body: Wow, what a great ending to this story! It's awesome that you were able to fix your controllers after all - that tip is surely useful for others, too. And thank you for your continued support, it's fantastic to hear you are having fun with the game :D

reviews.review_description: Great game if you're tired of shooters......nice change of pace!

I just stared playing and have fun playing only negative so far is on story mode when I got to the basement I couldnt go up so i had to go to main menu and continue every time. I'm hoping it doesnt continue to happen or I probably wont continue with story mode.

developer_response.body: Don't worry, the game always begins at the same place, because that is the entrance to the factory. You can go back up from the basement once you have restored power there, and your progress will be saved whenever you exit the story from the tablet menu. Aside from Story Mode, you can also choose Arcade Mode from the main menu, if you just want to play a quick round. You can find the official manual for the game here: https://tunermaxx.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/01/RRF_Game_Manual.pdf

reviews.review_description: Very cute characters, fun story, challenging puzzles and fun “chores” make this game an exciting experience.

Great mixture of "match 3" with a twist and puzzle adventure gameplay. Has absolutely the best throwing mechanics I witnessed in any VR game so far (and I have played quite a lot). The visuals are quite simple but look great nonetheless. The sense of height inside the building is really fun and exciting. I also loved the German voice of Dotti by GamingLadyNici! ;-)
As so often, I wish the story mode was a bit longer, but there are still some "side missions" to do, like finding all the fuses etc.
Played on "normal" difficulty and found it relatively easy, yet relaxing - which is a plus for me!
